back_translation: And it happened afterward, that David inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Shall I go up to one of the cities of Judah?' And the LORD said to him, 'Go up.' And David said, 'Where shall I go up?' And He said, 'To Hebron.'
And David went up there, and also his two wives, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.
And his men who were with him David brought up, each man with his house; and they dwelt in the cities of Hebron.
And the men of Judah came, and there they anointed David king over the house of Judah. And they told David, saying, 'The men of Jabesh-gilead were the ones who buried Saul.'
And David sent messengers to the men of Jabesh-gilead, and said to them, 'Blessed are you to the LORD, who have done this kindness with your lord, with Saul, and have buried him.
And now may the LORD do with you steadfast love and truth; and also I will do with you this good, because you have done this thing.
And now let your hands be strong, and be sons of valor; for your lord Saul is dead, and also me the house of Judah has anointed as king over them.'
And Abner son of Ner, the commander of the army that belonged to Saul, took Ish-bosheth son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.
And he made him king over the Gilead and over the Ashurite and over Jezreel and over Ephraim and over Benjamin and over all Israel.
A son of forty years was Ish-bosheth son of Saul when he became king over Israel, and two years he reigned; only the house of Judah was after David.
And the number of the days that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
And Abner son of Ner and the servants of Ish-bosheth son of Saul went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
And Joab son of Zeruiah and the servants of David went out, and met them at the pool of Gibeon together; and they sat down — these on the pool from this side, and these on the pool from that side.
And Abner said to Joab, 'Let the young men arise please and compete before us.' And Joab said, 'Let them arise.'
And they arose and passed over by number — twelve for Benjamin and for Ish-bosheth son of Saul, and twelve from the servants of David.
And each grasped his fellow by the head, and his sword into the side of his fellow, and they fell together. And he called that place Helkath-hazzurim, which is in Gibeon.
And the battle was very hard on that day, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated before the servants of David.
And there were there the three sons of Zeruiah: Joab and Abishai and Asahel. And Asahel was swift on his feet, like one of the gazelles that are in the field.
And Asahel pursued after Abner, and did not turn to go to the right or to the left from after Abner.
And Abner turned behind him, and said, 'Are you this — Asahel?' And he said, 'I am.'

And Abner said to him, 'Turn aside for yourself to your right or to your left, and seize for yourself one of the young men, and take for yourself his equipment.' But Asahel was unwilling to turn aside from after him.
And Abner added again to say to Asahel, 'Turn aside for yourself from after me; why should I strike you to the ground? And how could I lift my face to Joab your brother?'

And he refused to turn aside, and Abner struck him with the butt of the spear into the belly, and the spear went out behind him; and he fell there and died in his place. And it happened that all who came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died, stood still.
And Joab and Abishai pursued after Abner; and the sun was going down, and they came as far as the hill of Ammah, which is opposite Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.
And the sons of Benjamin gathered themselves after Abner, and they became one band, and stood on the top of one hill.

And Abner called to Joab and said, 'Shall the sword devour forever? Do you not know that it will be bitter in the end? And how long will you not say to the people to return from after their brothers?'
And Joab said, 'As God lives — that if you had not spoken, then surely from the morning the people would have gone up, each from after his brother.'
And Joab blew the trumpet, and all the people stood still, and they did not pursue any longer after Israel, and they did not add any longer to fight.
And Abner and his men went through the Arabah all that night, and crossed the Jordan, and went through all the Bithron, and came to Mahanaim.
And Joab returned from after Abner, and gathered all the people; and nineteen men of the servants of David were missing, and Asahel.
And the servants of David had struck — from Benjamin and from the men of Abner — three hundred and sixty men died.
And they lifted up Asahel, and buried him in the tomb of his father, which is in Bethlehem. And Joab and his men went all the night, and the light dawned on them in Hebron.
